feed.title: SAP Community - ABAP Development
feed.subtitle: ABAP Development Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Table KNVI ( Customer Master Tax Indicator ) getting updated with extra irrelevant rows
entry.content:
Hi SAP experts,

Table KNVI ( Customer Master Tax Indicator ) getting updated with extra irrelevant rows ( junk data ) for a particular customer once is few months not regularly.  What might be the reason for this ? 

Would be grateful for your replies. 

Thanks,

Devendra

entry.title: How to create a line chart in R3
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I'm looking for a solution on how to create a line chart in R3 system. Are there any classes/interfaces etc. that you can recommend?

Best regards,
Cassandra

entry.title: Having an issue calling BOPF multi Node instance ACTION via OData Model Functionimport
entry.content:
Hi Folks,

we have a scenario to call a BOPF Multi Node Instance Action(Approval) for the Invoices from FIORI List report application, we were successfully able to use the ODatamodel FunctionImport method for the single node BOPF->CDS View instance however when trying to use the MultiNode instance of the BOPF action, end up into error, Kindly guide what would be the best way to have the ODatamodel function Import method handling Multi Node Instance BOPF action.

entry.title: Table SWWUSERWI vs FuMo SAP_WAPI_CREATE_WORKLIST
entry.content:
Hi all, 

We are currently using the Function module SAP_WAPI_CREATE_WORKLIST to get the number of workitems for every user, This is however causing performance issues when executed for more than 50 thousand dialog users one by one. We just need a count of How many Work items a User has to process for every user.

Can we use table SWWUSERWI instead ? I have compared various cases, the number of work items returned by the table and the function modules are sometimes different, can we still use this table ?

or is there any other utility which could return the number of work items per user in one Go for all the users.

Thanks for any useful Hints. 

Best regards

Ketan

entry.title: Material Length - SAP Readiness Check
entry.content:
Hello,

The SAP Note 2215852 described how ABAP code should look in order to be consistent with the new material length. In the Note there is following code example:
"So the adapted code looks as follows:"

DATA: ls_matnr_selection TYPE bapimatram,
        lt_matnr_selection TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF bapimatram,
        lt_material_list   TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF bapimatlst,
        ls_material_list   TYPE bapimatlst.


  ls_matnr_selection-option         = 'CP'.
  ls_matnr_selection-sign           = 'I'.
  ls_matnr_selection-matnr_low_long = 'MAT_A*'.

  INSERT ls_matnr_selection INTO TABLE lt_matnr_selection.

  CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_MATERIAL_GETLIST'
    TABLES
      matnrselection = lt_matnr_selection
      matnrlist      = lt_material_list.

  SORT lt_material_list BY material_long.

  READ TABLE lt_material_list INTO ls_material_list INDEX 1.

  DATA: ls_material_general_data TYPE bapimatdoa,
        lv_old_mat_no            TYPE bismt.

  CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_MATERIAL_GET_DETAIL'
    EXPORTING
      material_long         = ls_material_list-material_long
    IMPORTING
      material_general_data = ls_material_general_data.

  lv_old_mat_no = ls_material_general_data-old_mat_no_long.

I have created a z-report with this and only this exact code and checked it with the Custom Code Analysis using Variant S4HANA_READINESS and I still receive the same findings (concerning lines 13 & 25) that one gets for unadapted code:

So SAP's suggested fix for these findings has no impact and since it is one of the most common findings, we need another way to fix them, ideally not through pragmas. Any suggestions are appreciated.

Thank you

entry.title: MRP-Type(Dismm) Field usage issue in Structure
entry.content:
Hello All

In custom structure where i have a field DISMM(MRP-type) which is causing an ATC P1 issue 
Short text : SAP S/4HANA Simplification Item SI_PP14: Logistics_PP-MRP - Forecast Based Plann
Note : 3211383
I have used this structure in my report and due to usage of it , i am getting an ATC issue in structure
I want to know how i can deal with this issue, i need a field DISMM in report which has been fetched from marc and displaying to user through ALV i can't remove the field i need it and i am unable to find a solution or else pragmas.
So can anyone who has worked on S4 Hana Migration help me out 

Thanks in Advance

#ABAP #S4 Hana #MRP Type #ATC #CCA  #SAP Technical  #MRP #dismm

entry.title: Sales Order Enhancement using Clean core approach using RAP
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

We want to determine tax at the line item level. Whenever we are creating or changing any line item data we want to trigger a logic to determine tax. We have already checked the blog From User Exits to RAP: Adopting a Clean Core Appr... - SAP Community but no event is triggering at the time of changing the data of line item before saving. Is there any other clean core approach which we can utilize for the development instead of user exits?

Any suggestions are helpful!

Thank you!

entry.title: Custom Fields Not Updating in BAPI_PO_CHANGE
entry.content:
I am currently working on a project where I need to update custom fields in a purchase order using the BAPI_PO_CHANGE function module. Despite my efforts, the custom fields isdind and branch in the EKKO table are not updating as expected. Below is the code I am using:

 

DATA: p_ebeln        TYPE ekko-ebeln,
      po_items       TYPE TABLE OF bapimepoitem,
      i_bapi_return  TYPE TABLE OF bapiret2,
      wa_header_data TYPE bapiekko,
      lt_extensionin TYPE TABLE OF bapiparex,
      ls_extensionin TYPE bapiparex,
      bapi_te_po     TYPE bapi_te_mepoheader,
      bapi_te_pox    TYPE bapi_te_mepoheaderx,
      lv_isdind      TYPE bapi_te_mepoheader-isdind.

DATA: lt_final       TYPE TABLE OF  ztglint012,
      ls_final       TYPE           ztglint012,
      lt_ekko        TYPE TABLE OF  ekko,
      ls_ekko        TYPE           ekko,
      lt_ekpo        TYPE TABLE OF  ekpo,
      ls_ekpo        TYPE           ekpo,
      po_item        TYPE TABLE OF  bapimepoitem,
      ls_po_item     TYPE           bapimepoitem,
      po_schedule    TYPE  TABLE OF bapimeposchedule,
      ls_po_schedule TYPE           bapimeposchedule,
      po_acc         TYPE TABLE OF  bapimepoaccount,
      ls_po_acc      TYPE           bapimepoaccount,
      po_services    TYPE TABLE OF  bapiesllc,
      ls_po_services TYPE           bapiesllc,
      lv_delivery_dt TYPE           sy-datum,
      lv_del_st_dt   TYPE           sy-datum,
      lv_del_ed_dt   TYPE           sy-datum,
      lv_count       TYPE           i,
      lv_packno      TYPE           packno.

DATA: po_itemx       TYPE TABLE OF bapimepoitemx,
      ls_po_itemx    TYPE           bapimepoitemx,
      po_schedx      TYPE TABLE OF bapimeposchedulx,
      ls_po_schedx   TYPE           bapimeposchedulx.

p_ebeln = '3320000061'. 

" Call the BAPI to get PO details
CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_PO_GETDETAIL1'
  EXPORTING
    purchaseorder = p_ebeln
    services      = 'X'
  TABLES
    poitem        = po_item
    poschedule    = po_schedule
    poaccount     = po_acc
    poservices    = po_services.

" Check for errors
IF sy-subrc <> 0.
  LOOP AT i_bapi_return INTO DATA(ls_return).
    IF ls_return-type = 'E'.
      WRITE: / 'Error:', ls_return-message.
      EXIT.
    ENDIF.
  ENDLOOP.
ENDIF.

DATA: ld_ponumber   TYPE ebeln,
      lt_return     TYPE TABLE OF bapiret2,
      it_extension  TYPE TABLE OF bapiparex,
      ext_container TYPE bapiparex.

ld_ponumber = '3320000061'. 

CLEAR it_extension[].

* Fill the custom fields for header
*bapi_te_po-po_number = ld_ponumber.
bapi_te_po-isdind = 'X'. " lv_isdind
bapi_te_po-branch = 'MAH'. " lv_branch

*bapi_te_pox-po_number = ld_ponumber.
bapi_te_pox-isdind = 'X'.
bapi_te_pox-branch = 'X'.

ext_container-structure = 'BAPI_TE_MEPOHEADER'.
cl_abap_container_utilities=>fill_container_c(
  EXPORTING
    im_value    = bapi_te_po
  IMPORTING
    ex_container = ext_container+30 ).
APPEND ext_container TO it_extension.

ext_container-structure = 'BAPI_TE_MEPOHEADERX'.
cl_abap_container_utilities=>fill_container_c(
  EXPORTING
    im_value    = bapi_te_pox
  IMPORTING
    ex_container = ext_container+30 ).
APPEND ext_container TO it_extension.

* Populate POITEMX and POSCHEDULEX
LOOP AT po_item INTO ls_po_item.
  ls_po_itemx-po_item = ls_po_item-po_item.
  ls_po_itemx-quantity = 'X'. " Example field
  APPEND ls_po_itemx TO po_itemx.
ENDLOOP.

LOOP AT po_schedule INTO ls_po_schedule.
  ls_po_schedx-po_item = ls_po_schedule-po_item.
  ls_po_schedx-sched_line = ls_po_schedule-sched_line.

  IF ls_po_schedule-po_item IS NOT INITIAL.
    ls_po_schedx-po_item = 'X'.
  ENDIF.
  IF ls_po_schedule-sched_line IS NOT INITIAL.
    ls_po_schedx-sched_line = 'X'.
  ENDIF.
  IF ls_po_schedule-del_datcat_ext IS NOT INITIAL.
    ls_po_schedx-del_datcat_ext = 'X'.
  ENDIF.
  IF ls_po_schedule-delivery_date IS NOT INITIAL.
    ls_po_schedx-delivery_date = 'X'.
  ENDIF.
  IF ls_po_schedule-quantity IS NOT INITIAL.
    ls_po_schedx-quantity = 'X'.
  ENDIF.
  IF ls_po_schedule-deliv_time IS NOT INITIAL.
    ls_po_schedx-deliv_time = 'X'.
  ENDIF.
  IF ls_po_schedule-stat_date IS NOT INITIAL.
    ls_po_schedx-stat_date = 'X'.
  ENDIF.
  IF ls_po_schedule-preq_item IS NOT INITIAL.
    ls_po_schedx-preq_item = 'X'.
  ENDIF.
  IF ls_po_schedule-po_date IS NOT INITIAL.
    ls_po_schedx-po_date = 'X'.
  ENDIF.
  APPEND ls_po_schedx TO po_schedx.
ENDLOOP.

* Call the BAPI to update the purchase order
CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_PO_CHANGE'
  EXPORTING
    purchaseorder = ld_ponumber
  TABLES
    return        = lt_return
    poitem        = po_item
    poitemx       = po_itemx
*    poschedule    = po_schedule
*    poschedx      = po_schedx
    poaccount     = po_acc
    poservices    = po_services
    extensionin   = it_extension.

* Commit the changes
CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT'
  EXPORTING
    wait = 'X'.

entry.title: Function module parameter not found
entry.content:
I have an odd situation with an Export parameter not being found in my include.

Here's the function declaration;

function z_ca_get_cluster.
*"----------------------------------------------------------------------
*"*"Local Interface:
*" IMPORTING
*" VALUE(IM_PROVIDER) TYPE ZCADEPARTMENT OPTIONAL
*" VALUE(IM_SUBPROVIDER) TYPE ZCASUBDEPT OPTIONAL
*" EXPORTING
*" REFERENCE(EX_FILE) TYPE FILE_TABLE. <--- Problem parameter
*" REFERENCE(EX_CLUSTERID) <--- Also not found
*"----------------------------------------------------------------------

clear: gt_recover.

Here's the includes;

*******************************************************************
* System-defined Include-files. *
*******************************************************************
include lzcaclustertop. " Global Declarations
include lzcaclusteruxx. " Function Modules

*******************************************************************
* User-defined Include-files (if necessary). *
*******************************************************************
include lzcaclusterlcl.

And here we are inside the include which has an issue;

class lcl_handler implementation.

method on_hotspot_click.
data: ld_value type zcafilename,
ld_row type lvc_s_row.
field-symbols: <row> type zcaclusterid.
sender->get_current_cell( importing e_value = ld_value es_row_id = ld_row ).

ex_file = ld_value.

...

endmethod.

endclass.

If I try to activate it, I get the following error;

Field "EX_FILE" is unknown.

I have tried activating the include by itself and I have tried activating everything together. I have tried deleting and readding the export parameters, but nothing seems to change this error. I have even tried forcing the activation but it short dumps when I try to run it.

entry.title: ABAP OOP Syntax help
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I'm having a problem accessing a table inside a method. I'm hoping someone can help formulate the correct syntax to accomplish this.

The scenario takes place after clicking on delete button of a custom class ALV. Through debug, I found only 1 variable (layout) accessible inside the method "Process_delete_records". I'd like to process the content of the table inside the method "Process_delete_records".

Here's the complete path of the table:

 

LAYOUT->FALV->CL_GUI_ALV_GRID_BASE->M_SELECTED_ROWS-ROW_ID

 

I attached some photos for your reference:

 

Hope someone can help me with this.

Thank you,

Geoff

entry.title: Standard transaction or Function Module to delete Stock Transfer Requisition
entry.content:
Hi experts!

I am creating an RFC to delete the Stock Transfer Requisitions in SAP APO system. Those documents are created via BAPI_POSRVAPS_SAVEMULTI3 and they want me to provide option to delete them using only its number (not version, product nor location). STRs numbers can be observed with transaction /n/SAPAPO/RRP3  but when I try to delete them using its number with report /SAPAPO/RLCDELETE I got message "No orders exist in LiveCache" (/SAPAPO/SNP055). 
Do you know any BAPI, class or utility program to delete Stock Transfer Requisitions? Or can you bring up a code snippet or a pseudocode about what data I need to get to delete those documents? Thank you

entry.title: Is it possible to use F4 help event of CL_GUI_ALV_GRID for CL_SALV_TABLE related program
entry.content:
Hi All,

I have created a program where I want to create custom F4 help depending on the line selected. Please find the code below requesting you to please let me know if there is any solution. I have tried to change the field catalog for two fields to make them editable, but that's not working. Please suggest a correction for this, too.

@Sandra_Rossi 

@naimesh_patel 

@former_member182550 

@raymond_giuseppi 

 

 

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& Report ZPPMAT_QRPRINBT
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*&
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
REPORT ZPPMAT_QRPRINBT.
TABLES : MSEG,MKPF,MARA,MAKT.
SELECTION-SCREEN : BEGIN OF BLOCK B1 WITH FRAME TITLE TEXT-H01.
  SELECT-OPTIONS: S_MBLNR FOR MSEG-MBLNR.
SELECTION-SCREEN : END OF BLOCK B1.

CLASS LCL_EVENT_HANDLER DEFINITION.
  PUBLIC SECTION.


    METHODS:
      ON_LINK_CLICK
        FOR EVENT
        LINK_CLICK OF CL_SALV_EVENTS_TABLE
        IMPORTING ROW COLUMN.
    METHODS ON_ADDED_FUNCTION
      FOR EVENT IF_SALV_EVENTS_FUNCTIONS~ADDED_FUNCTION OF CL_SALV_EVENTS_TABLE
      IMPORTING E_SALV_FUNCTION..


ENDCLASS.                    "lcl_event_handler DEFINITION

CLASS LCL_REPORT DEFINITION.
  PUBLIC SECTION .
*   ALV reference
    INTERFACES IF_ALV_RM_GRID_FRIEND .
    DATA: LO_FUNCTIONS TYPE REF TO CL_SALV_FUNCTIONS_LIST.
    DATA: LO_SELECTIONS TYPE REF TO CL_SALV_SELECTIONS.
    DATA: LO_SALV TYPE REF TO CL_SALV_TABLE.
    TYPES: BEGIN OF TY_MKPF,
             MBLNR TYPE MBLNR,
             MJAHR TYPE MJAHR,
             BLDAT TYPE BLDAT,
             BUDAT TYPE BUDAT,
             XBLNR TYPE XBLNR,
           END OF TY_MKPF.

    TYPES: BEGIN OF TY_MSEG,
             MBLNR      TYPE MBLNR,
             MJAHR      TYPE MJAHR,
             WERKS      TYPE MSEG-WERKS,
             MATNR      TYPE MATNR,
             CHARG      TYPE CHARG_D,
             LIFNR      TYPE LIFNR,
             MENGE      TYPE MENGE_D,
             MEINS      TYPE MEINS,
             EBELN      TYPE EBELN,
             ABLAD      TYPE ABLAD,
             VFDAT      TYPE VFDAT,
             HSDAT      TYPE HSDAT,
             BUDAT_MKPF TYPE BUDAT,
           END OF TY_MSEG.

    TYPES:BEGIN OF TY_FINAL,
            CHECK       TYPE YQRSTR-ZCHECK,
            MBLNR       TYPE YQRSTR-MBLNR,
            MJAHR       TYPE MSEG-MJAHR,
            WERKS       TYPE MSEG-WERKS,
            MATNR       TYPE YQRSTR-MATNR,
            MAKTX       TYPE YQRSTR-MAKTX,
            MENGE       TYPE YQRSTR-MENGE,
            MEINS       TYPE YQRSTR-MEINS,
            SKUN        TYPE YQRSTR-SKUN,
            QUAN        TYPE CHAR10,
            CREATED_BY  TYPE NAME1,
            VERIFIED_BY TYPE NAME1,
            BOX_UNIT    TYPE STRING,
          END OF TY_FINAL.

    DATA :IT_FINAL TYPE TABLE OF TY_FINAL,
          WA_FINAL TYPE  TY_FINAL.
    DATA: IT_MSEG    TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF TY_MSEG,
          LO_COLUMNS TYPE REF TO CL_SALV_COLUMNS,
          LO_COLUMN  TYPE REF TO CL_SALV_COLUMN_TABLE,
          LO_EVENT   TYPE REF TO CL_SALV_EVENTS_TABLE.


    TYPES : BEGIN OF TY_MARA,
              MATNR TYPE MARA-MATNR,
              FERTH TYPE MARA-FERTH,
            END OF TY_MARA.
    TYPES : BEGIN OF TY_MAKT,
              MATNR TYPE MAKT-MATNR,
              MAKTX TYPE MAKT-MAKTX,
              SPRAS TYPE MAKT-SPRAS,
            END OF TY_MAKT.

    DATA: IT_MKPF  TYPE TABLE OF  TY_MKPF,
          WA_MKPF  TYPE  TY_MKPF,
          IT_MSEG1 TYPE TABLE OF  TY_MSEG,
          WA_MSEG1 TYPE  TY_MSEG.
    DATA: IT_MARA TYPE TABLE OF TY_MARA,
          IT_MAKT TYPE TABLE OF TY_MAKT.

    DATA:
      LS_KEY    TYPE SALV_S_LAYOUT_KEY,
      LO_LAYOUT TYPE REF TO CL_SALV_LAYOUT.
    METHODS:
      HANDLE_ON_F4
        FOR EVENT ONF4 OF CL_GUI_ALV_GRID
        IMPORTING E_FIELDNAME
                  ES_ROW_NO
                  ER_EVENT_DATA.
    METHODS:
      DOUBLE_CLICK FOR EVENT DOUBLE_CLICK OF CL_GUI_ALV_GRID IMPORTING E_ROW
                                                                       E_COLUMN
                                                                       ES_ROW_NO.
    METHODS: EVH_AFTER_REFRESH FOR EVENT AFTER_REFRESH OF CL_GUI_ALV_GRID IMPORTING SENDER.
    METHODS : GET_DATA_AND_FILL_DATA,
      DISPLAY.
  PRIVATE SECTION.
    DATA: HANDLER_ADDED TYPE ABAP_BOOL.
ENDCLASS.

CLASS LCL_REPORT IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD GET_DATA_AND_FILL_DATA .
    SELECT  MBLNR MJAHR BLDAT BUDAT XBLNR FROM MKPF
          INTO TABLE IT_MKPF
          WHERE MBLNR IN S_MBLNR.

    IF IT_MKPF[] IS NOT INITIAL.
      SELECT MBLNR
             MJAHR
             WERKS
             MATNR
             CHARG
             LIFNR
             MENGE
             MEINS
             EBELN
             ABLAD
             VFDAT
             HSDAT
             BUDAT_MKPF
        FROM MSEG
        INTO TABLE IT_MSEG
        FOR ALL ENTRIES IN IT_MKPF
        WHERE MBLNR = IT_MKPF-MBLNR AND
              BWART = '101'.
    ENDIF.
    IF IT_MSEG IS NOT INITIAL.
      SELECT MATNR
             FERTH  FROM MARA INTO TABLE IT_MARA
             FOR ALL ENTRIES IN IT_MSEG WHERE MATNR = IT_MSEG-MATNR ." SKU Value

      SELECT MATNR
             MAKTX
             SPRAS FROM MAKT INTO TABLE IT_MAKT
        FOR ALL ENTRIES IN IT_MSEG WHERE MATNR = IT_MSEG-MATNR
                                     AND SPRAS = SY-LANGU .

    ENDIF.


    LOOP AT IT_MSEG INTO DATA(WA_MSEG).
      WA_FINAL-MBLNR = WA_MSEG-MBLNR.
      WA_FINAL-MJAHR = WA_MSEG-MJAHR.
      WA_FINAL-WERKS = WA_MSEG-WERKS.
      READ TABLE IT_MARA INTO DATA(WA_MARA) WITH KEY MATNR = WA_MSEG-MATNR.
      IF SY-SUBRC = 0 .
        WA_FINAL-MATNR = WA_MSEG-MATNR.
        READ TABLE IT_makt INTO DATA(WA_MAKT) WITH KEY MATNR = WA_MSEG-MATNR .
        IF  SY-SUBRC = 0 .
          WA_FINAL-MAKTX = WA_MAKT-MAKTX.
        ENDIF.
        WA_FINAL-MENGE = WA_MSEG-MENGE.
        WA_FINAL-MEINS = WA_MSEG-MEINS.
        READ TABLE IT_MARA INTO WA_MARA WITH KEY MATNR = WA_FINAL-MATNR .
        IF SY-SUBRC = 0 .
          WA_FINAL-SKUN = WA_MARA-FERTH.
          IF WA_FINAL-SKUN IS NOT INITIAL .
            WA_FINAL-QUAN = WA_FINAL-MENGE / WA_FINAL-SKUN.
          ENDIF.
        ENDIF.
      ENDIF.
      APPEND WA_FINAL TO IT_FINAL.
    ENDLOOP.

  ENDMETHOD .
  METHOD DISPLAY.
    TRY.
        CALL METHOD CL_SALV_TABLE=>FACTORY
          IMPORTING
            R_SALV_TABLE = LO_SALV
          CHANGING
            T_TABLE      = IT_FINAL[].

        IF lO_SALV IS BOUND .
          CALL METHOD LO_SALV->SET_SCREEN_STATUS
            EXPORTING
              REPORT        = SY-REPID
              PFSTATUS      = 'ZSTANDARD' "your PF Status name
              SET_FUNCTIONS = LO_SALV->C_FUNCTIONS_ALL.


          LS_KEY-REPORT = SY-REPID.
          LO_LAYOUT = LO_SALV->GET_LAYOUT( ).
          LO_LAYOUT->SET_SAVE_RESTRICTION( IF_SALV_C_LAYOUT=>RESTRICT_NONE ).
          LO_LAYOUT->SET_KEY( LS_KEY ).
          LO_LAYOUT->SET_DEFAULT( ABAP_TRUE ).

          LO_COLUMNS = LO_SALV->GET_COLUMNS( ).
          LO_COLUMNS->SET_OPTIMIZE( 'X' ).
          TRY.
              LO_COLUMN ?= LO_COLUMNS->GET_COLUMN( 'CHECK' ).
              LO_COLUMN->SET_VISIBLE( IF_SALV_C_BOOL_SAP=>TRUE ).
              LO_COLUMN->SET_LONG_TEXT( 'Check' ).
              LO_COLUMN->SET_CELL_TYPE( IF_SALV_C_CELL_TYPE=>CHECKBOX_HOTSPOT ).
            CATCH CX_SALV_NOT_FOUND.
            CATCH CX_SALV_EXISTING.
            CATCH CX_SALV_DATA_ERROR.
          ENDTRY.
          TRY.
              LO_COLUMN ?= LO_COLUMNS->GET_COLUMN( 'QUAN' ).
              LO_COLUMN->SET_VISIBLE( IF_SALV_C_BOOL_SAP=>TRUE ).
              LO_COLUMN->SET_LONG_TEXT( 'Bag Quantity' ).
            CATCH CX_SALV_NOT_FOUND.
            CATCH CX_SALV_EXISTING.
            CATCH CX_SALV_DATA_ERROR.
          ENDTRY.
          TRY.
              LO_COLUMN ?= LO_COLUMNS->GET_COLUMN( 'CREATED_BY' ).
              LO_COLUMN->SET_VISIBLE( IF_SALV_C_BOOL_SAP=>TRUE ).
              LO_COLUMN->SET_LONG_TEXT( 'Inspected By' ).
              LO_COLUMN->SET_OPTIMIZED(
                VALUE = IF_SALV_C_BOOL_SAP=>TRUE
              ).
            CATCH CX_SALV_NOT_FOUND.
            CATCH CX_SALV_EXISTING.
            CATCH CX_SALV_DATA_ERROR.
          ENDTRY.
          TRY.
              LO_COLUMN ?= LO_COLUMNS->GET_COLUMN( 'VERIFIED_BY' ). " Find the 'MAKTX' column ans change attributes
              LO_COLUMN->SET_VISIBLE( IF_SALV_C_BOOL_SAP=>TRUE ).
              LO_COLUMN->SET_LONG_TEXT( 'Verified By' ).
              LO_COLUMN->SET_OPTIMIZED(
                VALUE = IF_SALV_C_BOOL_SAP=>TRUE
              ).
            CATCH CX_SALV_NOT_FOUND.
            CATCH CX_SALV_EXISTING.
            CATCH CX_SALV_DATA_ERROR.
          ENDTRY.
          TRY.
              LO_COLUMN ?= LO_COLUMNS->GET_COLUMN( 'BOX_UNIT' ). " Find the 'MAKTX' column ans change attributes
              LO_COLUMN->SET_VISIBLE( IF_SALV_C_BOOL_SAP=>TRUE ).
              LO_COLUMN->SET_LONG_TEXT( 'Box Unit' ).
              LO_COLUMN->SET_F4(
                VALUE = IF_SALV_C_BOOL_SAP=>TRUE
              ).
              LO_COLUMN->SET_OPTIMIZED(
                VALUE = IF_SALV_C_BOOL_SAP=>TRUE
              ).
            CATCH CX_SALV_NOT_FOUND.
            CATCH CX_SALV_EXISTING.
            CATCH CX_SALV_DATA_ERROR.
          ENDTRY.
          DATA LO_SETTINGS TYPE REF TO CL_SALV_DISPLAY_SETTINGS.
          LO_SETTINGS = LO_SALV->GET_DISPLAY_SETTINGS( ).
          LO_SETTINGS->SET_STRIPED_PATTERN( IF_SALV_C_BOOL_SAP=>TRUE ).

          DATA: LO_EVENTS TYPE REF TO CL_SALV_EVENTS_TABLE,
                G_GRID    TYPE REF TO CL_GUI_ALV_GRID.
          LO_EVENTS = LO_SALV->GET_EVENT( ).

          DATA: LO_EVENT_HANDLER TYPE REF TO LCL_EVENT_HANDLER.
          CREATE OBJECT LO_EVENT_HANDLER.

          SET HANDLER HANDLE_ON_F4 FOR ALL INSTANCES.
          SET HANDLER DOUBLE_CLICK FOR ALL INSTANCES.
          DATA(SELECTIONS) = LO_SALV->GET_SELECTIONS( ).
          SELECTIONS->SET_SELECTION_MODE(   IF_SALV_C_SELECTION_MODE=>CELL  ). "Single row selection
          SET HANDLER LO_EVENT_HANDLER->ON_LINK_CLICK FOR LO_EVENTS.
          SET HANDLER LO_EVENT_HANDLER->ON_ADDED_FUNCTION FOR LO_EVENTS.

*          DATA :MO_EDIT  TYPE REF TO IF_SALV_GUI_OM_EDIT_RESTRICTED.
*          MO_EDIT = LO_SALV->EXTENDED_GRID_API( )->EDITABLE_RESTRICTED( ).
*
*          TRY.
*              MO_EDIT->SET_ATTRIBUTES_FOR_COLUMNNAME(
*                EXPORTING
*                  COLUMNNAME              = 'VERIFIED_BY'
*                  ALL_CELLS_INPUT_ENABLED = ABAP_TRUE
*              ).
*            CATCH CX_SALV_NOT_FOUND. " ALV: General Error Class (Checked in Syntax Check)
*          ENDTRY.
*          TRY.
*              MO_EDIT->SET_ATTRIBUTES_FOR_COLUMNNAME(
*                EXPORTING
*                  COLUMNNAME              = 'CREATED_BY'
*                  ALL_CELLS_INPUT_ENABLED = ABAP_TRUE
*              ).
*            CATCH CX_SALV_NOT_FOUND. " ALV: General Error Class (Checked in Syntax Check)
*          ENDTRY.
          LO_SALV->DISPLAY( ).
        ENDIF .
      CATCH CX_ROOT .

    ENDTRY.

  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD HANDLE_ON_F4.
    BREAK-POINT.
  ENDMETHOD.
  METHOD DOUBLE_CLICK.
    BREAK-POINT.
  ENDMETHOD.
  METHOD EVH_AFTER_REFRESH.
    CHECK HANDLER_ADDED EQ ABAP_FALSE.
*    SET HANDLER ME->EVH_DEL_CHANGE_SELECTION FOR SENDER.

*    "to use this method you need to inherit from cl_gui_alv_grid or add interface IF_ALV_RM_GRID_FRIEND
*    "to your class
    SENDER->SET_DELAY_CHANGE_SELECTION(
      EXPORTING
        TIME   = 100    " Time in Milliseconds
      EXCEPTIONS
        ERROR  = 1
        OTHERS = 2
    ).
    IF SY-SUBRC <> 0.
*     message id sy-msgid type sy-msgty number sy-msgno
*                with sy-msgv1 sy-msgv2 sy-msgv3 sy-msgv4.
    ENDIF.

    SENDER->REGISTER_DELAYED_EVENT(
      EXPORTING
        I_EVENT_ID = SENDER->MC_EVT_DELAYED_CHANGE_SELECT
      EXCEPTIONS
        ERROR      = 1
        OTHERS     = 2
    ).
    IF SY-SUBRC <> 0.
    ENDIF.

    SENDER->GET_FRONTEND_FIELDCATALOG(
      IMPORTING
        ET_FIELDCATALOG = DATA(FCAT)    " Field Catalog
    ).

    "setting editable fields
    ASSIGN FCAT[ FIELDNAME = 'VERIFIED_BY' ] TO FIELD-SYMBOL(<FCAT>).
    IF SY-SUBRC EQ 0.
      <FCAT>-EDIT = ABAP_TRUE.
    ENDIF.

    ASSIGN FCAT[ FIELDNAME = 'CREATED_BY' ] TO <FCAT>.
    IF SY-SUBRC EQ 0.
      <FCAT>-EDIT = ABAP_TRUE.
    ENDIF.

    SENDER->SET_FRONTEND_FIELDCATALOG( IT_FIELDCATALOG = FCAT ).
    SENDER->REGISTER_EDIT_EVENT(
      EXPORTING
        I_EVENT_ID = SENDER->MC_EVT_MODIFIED    " Event ID
*      exceptions
*       error      = 1
*       others     = 2
    ).
    IF SY-SUBRC <> 0.
*     message id sy-msgid type sy-msgty number sy-msgno
*                with sy-msgv1 sy-msgv2 sy-msgv3 sy-msgv4.
    ENDIF.

    SENDER->SET_READY_FOR_INPUT(
      I_READY_FOR_INPUT = 1
    ).

    HANDLER_ADDED = ABAP_TRUE.
    SENDER->REFRESH_TABLE_DISPLAY(
*      exporting
*        is_stable      =     " With Stable Rows/Columns
*        i_soft_refresh =     " Without Sort, Filter, etc.
*      exceptions
*        finished       = 1
*        others         = 2
    ).
    IF SY-SUBRC <> 0.
*     message id sy-msgid type sy-msgty number sy-msgno
*                with sy-msgv1 sy-msgv2 sy-msgv3 sy-msgv4.
    ENDIF.


  ENDMETHOD.


ENDCLASS.

START-OF-SELECTION.
  DATA: LO_REPORT TYPE REF TO LCL_REPORT.
  CREATE OBJECT LO_REPORT.
  LO_REPORT->GET_DATA_AND_FILL_DATA( ).
  LO_REPORT->DISPLAY( ).

CLASS LCL_EVENT_HANDLER IMPLEMENTATION.

  METHOD ON_LINK_CLICK.
    DATA :MO_EDIT  TYPE REF TO IF_SALV_GUI_OM_EDIT_RESTRICTED.
    FIELD-SYMBOLS: <LFA_DATA> LIKE LINE OF LO_REPORT->IT_FINAl.
    READ TABLE LO_REPORT->IT_FINAL ASSIGNING <LFA_DATA> INDEX ROW.
    CHECK SY-SUBRC IS INITIAL.
    IF <LFA_DATA>-CHECK IS INITIAL.
      <LFA_DATA>-CHECK = 'X'.
    ELSE.
      CLEAR <LFA_DATA>-CHECK.
    ENDIF.
    LO_REPORT->LO_SALV->REFRESH( ).
  ENDMETHOD.                    "on_link_click
  METHOD ON_ADDED_FUNCTION.
    DATA: IT_BAPIRET2 TYPE TABLE OF BAPIRET2,
          WA_BAPIRET2 TYPE BAPIRET2.

    DATA: LO_GRID TYPE REF TO CL_GUI_ALV_GRID.
    IF LO_GRID IS INITIAL.
      CALL FUNCTION 'GET_GLOBALS_FROM_SLVC_FULLSCR'
        IMPORTING
          E_GRID = LO_GRID.
    ENDIF.

    CALL METHOD LO_GRID->CHECK_CHANGED_DATA.

    LOOP AT LO_REPORT->IT_FINAL  INTO DATA(WA_FINAL) WHERE CHECK = 'X'.
      IF WA_FINAL-CREATED_BY IS  INITIAL .
        WA_BAPIRET2-TYPE ='E'.
        WA_BAPIRET2-MESSAGE = |Please Maintain Created By For Material Doc { WA_FINAL-MBLNR }|.
        APPEND WA_BAPIRET2 TO IT_BAPIRET2 .
        CONTINUE.
      ENDIF .
      IF WA_FINAL-VERIFIED_BY IS  INITIAL .
        WA_BAPIRET2-TYPE ='E'.
        WA_BAPIRET2-MESSAGE = |Please Maintain Verified By For Material Doc { WA_FINAL-MBLNR }|.
        APPEND WA_BAPIRET2 TO IT_BAPIRET2 .
        CONTINUE.
      ENDIF .
    ENDLOOP.
    "perform something.

  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

entry.title: BAPI_BOM_UPLOAD_SAVE VALID_DATE ERROR
entry.content:
Dear Expert

I use BAPI - BAPI_BOM_UPLOAD_SAVE to create a BOM. From Postman transfer data to SAP BAPI.

But Valid_FROM always is Today. I can not use other Date.

Is there any expert could tell me the root cause?

thanks for your help !!

Best Regards,

Connie

entry.title: LSMW for OBCB
entry.content:
Hi,

I am creating LSMW for transaction OBCB with Batch input option.

In the first pop-up( BDC recording (SAPLSVIX 0100 ) , I give the partner type, Partner number and company code. press enter.

In the next screen,( BDC recording (SAPL0F11 0061 ) I click on new entries,  Goods service number,  text, Gl a/c and company code.  and press save.  

click on up arrow button. 

I am expecting this process to repeat in the LSMW; but the BDC is failing as it is expecting entries in screen 61 and not going back to selection screen. 

Anyone tried this before, can let me know what to do ? Thanks

entry.title: ABAP CDS Views: Value Help Based on Foreign Key Relationship
entry.content:
Introduction of CDS Views:

CDS (Core Data Services) views are a key concept in SAP's data modeling framework, designed to define, consume, and manage data in a more efficient and flexible way. CDS views provide an abstraction layer over the database tables and allow for a more structured and standardized approach to working with data within SAP applications, especially in the context of SAP S/4HANA and SAP HANA systems.

Key Characteristics of CDS Views:

  • Declarative Data Modeling: CDS views allow you to define the data model using a declarative syntax, focusing on what data is needed rather than how to retrieve it.
  • SQL-based: They are based on SQL, making them highly optimized for performance and leveraging the power of the SAP HANA database.
  • Layer of Abstraction: CDS views abstract the complexity of direct database table interactions, which simplifies data consumption for application developers and users.
  • Integration with SAP Fiori and Analytics: CDS views are deeply integrated with SAP Fiori, SAP Gateway, and other SAP applications, making it easier to expose data for reporting and analytics.

Types of CDS Views:

There are mainly two types of CDS views:

  1. Basic CDS Views:
    • These are simple views that pull data from one or more database tables or other views. They are typically used to define basic data structures that will be further extended or consumed by other views or applications.
  2. Consumption CDS Views:
    • These are used to expose data for consumption in applications or analytics tools. They often involve annotations for specifying how the data should be presented in user interfaces (e.g., SAP Fiori apps), or for integration with reporting tools.
  3. Composite CDS Views:
    • These combine multiple basic views or tables and provide a more complex dataset. They are useful for aggregating data or creating more intricate data models.
  4. Parameterized CDS Views:
    • These accept parameters, making the view flexible and reusable for different input values.

In SAP, ensuring an efficient and user-friendly experience for entering and selecting data is crucial for business processes. One of the most used features for this purpose is F4 Value Help. This feature provides users with a list of possible values to choose from, making data entry faster, more accurate, and less prone to errors.

With the introduction of Core Data Services (CDS) Views in SAP, F4 Value Help has become even more powerful, enabling seamless integration with advanced data models. In this blog, we’ll dive into how F4 value help works in CDS Views. So, let’s begin…

Working of F4 Value Help

Here we have created a basic CDS view with all the required field. On top of it we have a composite view with all the required annotations, and on top of composite view we have a consumption view. Let’s go into details in each view with the screenshots below:

Here, I have created a basic view using ‘C_EWM_WarehouseTaskLeanQ’. As per the requirement, many other fields were needed along with Warehouse and date fields. In the basic view, we can select all the required field and even can make some arithmetic calculations. 

 

Once the basic view is created, we must create a composite view. In the composite view, we select all the fields of basic view along with the association on the F4 help CDS view, which is ‘I_EWMWarehouseNumber’ in this case. All the annotations required for the composite view and the F4 value help are mentioned below:

For Composite view:

@vdm: {viewType : #COMPOSITE } – to declare view as composite

@analytics.dataCategory: #CUBE - defines a cube view. This means it can be consumed by a CDS Analytical Projection View. The fields of a cube view can play the role of either a dimension or a measure.

For F4 value help:

@search.defaultSearchElement: true - Specifies that the element is to be considered in a freestyle search.

@search.fuzzinessThreshold: 0.8 - Specifies the least level of fuzziness (with regard to some comparison criteria passed at runtime) the element must have to be considered in a fuzzy search at all.

@ObjectModel.foreignKey.association:'_WarehouseF4Help' - work with primary key of CDS view used in association. Here we must provide the direct mapping to the text view (I_EWM_WarehouseNumber). Also, expose the association as it is done in the snap below.

In the consumption view, below annotations are must for F4 value help to work.

@ObjectModel.foreignKey.association: '_WarehouseF4Help' – provides the foreign key relation to the F4 help view.

@Consumption.valueHelp: '_WarehouseF4Help.Warehouse' – links to the required field in the F4 help view.

Below are the snaps of UI5 application where we have F4 help enabled.

 

Conclusion:

ABAP CDS views are crucial for enhancing data consumption and providing a user-friendly experience in SAP applications. The F4 Value Help based on foreign key relationships allows users to select values with ease, improving efficiency and reducing errors. This feature is especially beneficial when integrating complex data models in SAP S/4HANA environments, where the correct values are required for proper data entry.

References:

Value help based on foreign key relationship.

ObjectModel Annotation.

AnalyticsDetails Annotations.

entry.title: Find the tables that use the structure BAPI_TE_WBS_ELEMENT
entry.content:
Good Morning,

I have recently upgrade an S4 system 2021FPS02 -> 2023FPS03.

SPDD reported that the structure BAPI_TE_WBS_ELEMENT had been modified in the system.
In order to know if a Reset could imply loosing columns in tables, I would like to list which tables do use this structure.

What is the easiest way to find the Tables that uses this structure ?

Have a nice day.

entry.title: using selection screen tabbed block how to bind SALV, OOAOV,ALV ??
entry.content:
I Want to Bind ALV to Tabbed block. any suggestion might Help me.

it is not showing in tabbed block, instead it shows in different screen if you click any other tabbed block and come back again.

I am working on an SAP ABAP report that uses a tabbed selection-screen block to display different datasets (e.g., Purchase Orders, Inbound Deliveries, Payment Details, etc.). However, when I use CL_SALV_TABLE to display an ALV report inside a tab, it opens in a new screen instead of displaying within the tabbed block.

Here is the simplified structure of my code:

  • I define selection-screen tabs with subscreens (100–112).

  • When a tab is selected, it should display an ALV report within the same tab.

  • Currently, my ALV grid opens in a new full screen, which is not the expected behavior.

I have tried:

  • Using CL_SALV_TABLE but it does not display inside the tab.

  • Creating a subscreen but struggling to integrate ALV within it.

How can I display the ALV output inside the tabbed block (not a separate screen) using CL_SALV_TABLE or CL_GUI_ALV_GRID?

Any guidance, corrections, or best practices would be greatly appreciated!

DATA: lv_lifnr   TYPE lfa1-lifnr,
      lt_po_data TYPE TABLE OF ekko,
      lt_po_item TYPE TABLE OF ekpo,
      lt_inbound TYPE TABLE OF mseg,
      lt_payment TYPE TABLE OF bseg.

DATA: lo_salv TYPE REF TO cl_salv_table.

SELECT-OPTIONS: so_vndr FOR lv_lifnr.

SELECTION-SCREEN BEGIN OF TABBED BLOCK tabs FOR 18 LINES.

  SELECTION-SCREEN: TAB (20) tab1 USER-COMMAND fc1 DEFAULT SCREEN 100,
  TAB (20) tab2 USER-COMMAND fc2 DEFAULT SCREEN 101,
  TAB (20) tab3 USER-COMMAND fc3 DEFAULT SCREEN 102,
  TAB (20) tab4 USER-COMMAND fc4 DEFAULT SCREEN 103,
  TAB (20) tab5 USER-COMMAND fc5 DEFAULT SCREEN 104,
  TAB (20) tab6 USER-COMMAND fc6 DEFAULT SCREEN 105,
  TAB (20) tab7 USER-COMMAND fc7 DEFAULT SCREEN 106,
  TAB (20) tab8 USER-COMMAND fc8 DEFAULT SCREEN 107,
  TAB (20) tab9 USER-COMMAND fc9 DEFAULT SCREEN 108,
  TAB (20) tab10 USER-COMMAND fc10 DEFAULT SCREEN 109,
  TAB (20) tab11 USER-COMMAND fc11 DEFAULT SCREEN 110,
  TAB (20) tab12 USER-COMMAND fc12 DEFAULT SCREEN 111,
  TAB (20) tab13 USER-COMMAND fc13 DEFAULT SCREEN 112.

SELECTION-SCREEN END OF BLOCK tabs.

SELECTION-SCREEN BEGIN OF SCREEN 100 AS SUBSCREEN.

SELECTION-SCREEN END OF SCREEN 100.

SELECTION-SCREEN BEGIN OF SCREEN 101 AS SUBSCREEN.

SELECTION-SCREEN END OF SCREEN 101.

SELECTION-SCREEN BEGIN OF SCREEN 102 AS SUBSCREEN.

SELECTION-SCREEN END OF SCREEN 102.

SELECTION-SCREEN BEGIN OF SCREEN 103 AS SUBSCREEN.

SELECTION-SCREEN END OF SCREEN 103.

SELECTION-SCREEN BEGIN OF SCREEN 104 AS SUBSCREEN.

SELECTION-SCREEN END OF SCREEN 104.

SELECTION-SCREEN BEGIN OF SCREEN 105 AS SUBSCREEN.

SELECTION-SCREEN END OF SCREEN 105.

SELECTION-SCREEN BEGIN OF SCREEN 106 AS SUBSCREEN.

SELECTION-SCREEN END OF SCREEN 106.

SELECTION-SCREEN BEGIN OF SCREEN 107 AS SUBSCREEN.

SELECTION-SCREEN END OF SCREEN 107.

SELECTION-SCREEN BEGIN OF SCREEN 108 AS SUBSCREEN.

SELECTION-SCREEN END OF SCREEN 108.

SELECTION-SCREEN BEGIN OF SCREEN 109 AS SUBSCREEN.

SELECTION-SCREEN END OF SCREEN 109.

SELECTION-SCREEN BEGIN OF SCREEN 110 AS SUBSCREEN.

SELECTION-SCREEN END OF SCREEN 110.

SELECTION-SCREEN BEGIN OF SCREEN 111 AS SUBSCREEN.

SELECTION-SCREEN END OF SCREEN 111.

SELECTION-SCREEN BEGIN OF SCREEN 112 AS SUBSCREEN.

SELECTION-SCREEN END OF SCREEN 112.

INITIALIZATION.

  tab1 = 'Purchase Orders'.
  tab2 = 'Inbound Delivery'.
  tab3 = 'Payment Details'.
  tab4 = 'Vendor Master'.
  tab5 = 'Goods Receipt'.
  tab6 = 'Invoice Receipt'.
  tab7 = 'Open PO Items'.
  tab8 = 'Closed PO Items'.
  tab9 = 'PO History'.
  tab10 = 'Invoice List'.
  tab11 = 'Payment Status'.
  tab12 = 'Goods Movement'.
  tab13 = 'Supplier Evaluation'.

* Sample code to populate internal tables (would be based on logic)

*  Event handling for tab selection (user command)

AT SELECTION-SCREEN.

*AT USER-COMMAND.
  CASE sy-ucomm.
    WHEN 'FC1'.  " Tab 1 - Purchase Order Details
      PERFORM display_po_data.
    WHEN 'FC2'.  " Tab 2 - Inbound Delivery
      " Add logic for Inbound Delivery
    WHEN 'FC3'.  " Tab 3 - Payment Details
      " Add logic for Payment Details
  ENDCASE.

FORM display_po_data.
*   Get Purchase Orders based on selected vendor(s)
  SELECT * FROM ekko
    INTO TABLE lt_po_data
  UP TO 30 ROWS.
  TRY.
* Create and display the salv grid
      cl_salv_table=>factory(
        IMPORTING
          r_salv_table = lo_salv
        CHANGING
          t_table      = lt_po_data ).

* Set the subscreen area for Tab 1 (Purchase Orders)
* lo_salv->get_functions( )->set_screen( 100 ).  " Set the screen for Tab 1

      lo_salv->display( ).

    CATCH cx_salv_msg INTO DATA(lx_msg).
      WRITE: / 'Error displaying Purchase Orders: ', lx_msg->get_text( ).
  ENDTRY.
ENDFORM.

entry.title: ITAB to JSON
entry.content:
Hello together,

I have to itabs and I want to serialze them into one JSON. Itab1 has the following fields (EquiNr, Matnr), itab2 has the following fields (OrderNr, Matnr).

With which class/method can I serialize to JSON that the JSON looks like:

{

"Itab1": [

{
"Equipmentnummer": 547891,
"Materialnummer": "0066897",
},
{
"Equipmentnummer": 547892,
"Materialnummer": "0066897",
}
],


"Itab2": [
{
"FaufNr": 275114221,
"Materialnummer": "00982145"
},
{
"FaufNr": 275114222,
"Materialnummer": "00982145"
},

],

"success": true,
"msg": "ok"
}

Best regards,

Stefan

entry.title: ADT Code Completion is Slow
entry.content:
Hello all,

I just started working with Eclipse + ADT and noticed that the code completion is really quite slow. It takes ~ 300ms for the next keyword to show up.

Coming from SE38 this is a HUGE step down, performance-wise and also with regards to the user experience, since in SE38 the next keyword was suggested instantly.

With Eclipse I program twice as slow now, compared to SE38 simply because of the massive wait times I have to endure and because I have gotten used to the snappy-ness of SE38 causing me to press tab before the code-completion pop up had a chance to show up.

Since I can only barely type 'field-symbol' in the time it would otherwise take for the suggestion to pop up, I wonder, is there a quick fix (pun intended) for this, to reduce the pop up times?

I realize, Eclipse does provide this funtionallity, but only for languages such as C++ or Java.

entry.title: SUSG integration with ATC
entry.content:
Hello All - there are soo many tcodes/ tool within SAP for performing analysis of Sap ECC custom code to S4 Hana conversion , which one is most preferred one ?

custom code  -  Analyze the custom code to S4 using ATC - ? or other SAP Readiness check2.0  /SDF/? 

usage of custom code - SUSG , SCMON , UPL - ? other ?

are there any sap ecc related fiori app to merge information of custom code along with usage ? - like CCMC / CCLM in solution manager ?

 

thanks
